architectural_decision.decision: Break the locked v2 curriculum and rebuild around the Report 1 phase-based zero-to-software-engineer path.
architectural_decision.v2_policy: curriculum.v2.json is frozen legacy import data, not the active source of truth.
architectural_decision.core_rule: Keep good lessons, break the old path, optimize for learner progression, proof, documentation quality, and job readiness.
architectural_decision.full_bundle_policy: Split files are authoritative. curriculum.v3.full.generated.json is an optional generated snapshot for review/export only.

projects.title: Shell + Git Lab
projects.summary: Build and navigate a real shell environment, then manage code changes through a complete Git workflow.
deliverables: Working implementation of Shell + Git Lab

tasks.title: Set up and navigate the terminal
tasks.instruction: Open your terminal, verify shell access, navigate directories, create and delete files, and configure basic shell aliases.

tasks.title: Initialize and manage a Git repository
tasks.instruction: Initialize a Git repository, stage and commit changes, create branches, merge, and resolve a simulated merge conflict.

tasks.title: Explain your workflow
tasks.instruction: Document your shell and Git workflow, including commands used, decisions made, and how you resolved the merge conflict.

rubric.description: Shell commands execute correctly; Git repository has proper commit history, branches, and conflict resolution.
rubric.title: Correctness

rubric.description: Workflow follows best practices: meaningful commits, sensible branching, clean command structure.
rubric.title: Process quality

rubric.description: Git log, branch graph, and documentation demonstrate a complete and correct workflow.
rubric.title: Proof

rubric.description: Clear explanation of shell and Git commands, including why specific approaches were chosen.
rubric.title: Explanation

verification.expected_output: Project meets rubric and proves module outcomes.

portfolio.artifact_description: Shell + Git Lab can be used as a learning proof artifact.

projects.title: Tooling Failure Lab
projects.summary: Install Go tooling, then deliberately introduce and diagnose common Go setup failures.
deliverables: Working implementation of Tooling Failure Lab

tasks.title: Install and configure Go
tasks.instruction: Install Go, set up GOPATH or workspace, verify the installation with go version, and run a hello-world program.

tasks.title: Introduce and diagnose tooling failures
tasks.instruction: Simulate common tooling failures: wrong PATH, broken GOPATH, missing dependencies, and module resolution errors. Diagnose each failure using available tooling output.

tasks.title: Document your troubleshooting
tasks.instruction: Write a troubleshooting guide documenting each failure, the diagnostic steps taken, and the resolution.

rubric.description: Go installation is correct; failure simulations are realistic and resolvable.
rubric.title: Correctness

rubric.description: Systematic diagnostic approach: identifying symptoms, isolating causes, applying fixes.
rubric.title: Diagnostic quality

verification.expected_output: Project meets rubric and proves module outcomes.

portfolio.artifact_description: Tooling Failure Lab can be used as a learning proof artifact.

projects.title: Performance Analysis Lab
projects.summary: Profile a Go application with pprof, identify CPU and memory bottlenecks, and apply targeted optimizations with measurable improvements.

tasks.title: Profile CPU usage with pprof
tasks.instruction: Run pprof CPU profiling on a provided Go application, generate a flame graph, and identify the top 3 CPU hotspots.

tasks.title: Analyze memory allocations
tasks.instruction: Use pprof heap profiling and escape analysis to identify excessive allocations and objects escaping to the heap.

tasks.title: Optimize and benchmark
tasks.instruction: Apply targeted optimizations (object pooling, slice preallocation, escape reduction) and measure before/after with benchmarks.

rubric.description: Correct identification of CPU and memory bottlenecks with pprof flame graphs and heap profiles.
rubric.title: Profiling accuracy

rubric.description: Optimizations target the real bottlenecks and follow Go performance best practices.
rubric.title: Optimization quality

rubric.description: Before/after benchmark data with statistical significance and profiling evidence.
rubric.title: Proof

rubric.description: Clear explanation of profiling process, bottleneck analysis, and optimization rationale.
rubric.title: Explanation

manual_steps: Automated: before/after benchmark comparison. Manual review: flame graph analysis and optimization report.
verification.expected_output: Project meets rubric and proves the target outcomes.

portfolio.artifact_description: Performance Analysis Lab can be used as a proof artifact.

projects.title: Advanced Electives Capstone
projects.summary: Apply advanced Go topics from the electives path: build a project demonstrating functional programming, systems programming, or cloud-native Go patterns.

tasks.title: Choose and plan an elective topic
tasks.instruction: Select an advanced Go topic from the electives, define a project scope, and outline the implementation plan.

tasks.title: Implement the project
tasks.instruction: Build a working Go application that demonstrates your chosen elective topic with proper packages, tests, and documentation.

tasks.title: Present your work
tasks.instruction: Write a technical summary explaining the advanced concepts used, design decisions, and what you learned.

rubric.description: Project demonstrates advanced Go concepts from the chosen elective with correct implementation.
rubric.title: Technical depth

rubric.description: Code follows Go idioms, is well-structured, tested, and documented.
rubric.title: Code quality

rubric.description: Tests, benchmarks, or demonstrations verify the implementation works correctly.
rubric.title: Proof

rubric.description: Clear technical write-up explaining the advanced concepts, design choices, and trade-offs.
rubric.title: Explanation

deliverables: Working Go project on elective topic

manual_steps: Manual review of project implementation, tests, and technical write-up against elective module criteria.
verification.expected_output: Project meets rubric and proves the target outcomes.

portfolio.artifact_description: Advanced Electives Capstone can be used as a proof artifact.
